Common_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Homo sapiens; Mus musculus; Danio rerio
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Homo sapiens; Mus musculus; Danio rerio
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Regulates angiogenesis, vascular permeability/barrier integrity, endothelial survival, vascular maturation
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Blood; serum/plasma; extracellular milieu around vascular cells
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes S1P-dependent endothelial survival, angiogenic behavior, vascular stabilization, and some VSMC proliferation/migration
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Originally isolated as an inducible gene from endothelial cells
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Endothelial cells; pup intimal vascular smooth muscle cells; cardiomyocytes
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Gi; Rac; VE-cadherin; integrins; PDGF receptor-β (proposed downstream relation)
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: S1P treatment; FTY720-phosphate; ischemia/reperfusion; inflammatory and vascular leak conditions
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. S1P1 is a principal endothelial S1P receptor promoting migration, morphogenesis, barrier integrity, and survival. 2. Endothelial-specific deletion phenocopies global S1p1 knockout, demonstrating an essential role in vascular maturation/stabilization. 3. In VSMCs, S1P1 supports proliferation and migration of intimal-type cells.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene knockout; tissue-specific knockout; receptor agonist studies; in vitro endothelial and VSMC assays

Common_Genes.Source_Species: Human; Mouse; Zebrafish-like homolog (Miles Apart most similar)
Common_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Homo sapiens; Mus musculus; Danio rerio
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Homo sapiens; Mus musculus; Danio rerio
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Controls vascular tone, migration restraint, developmental signaling
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Often opposes S1P1 by activating Rho/G13 and inhibiting Rac, thereby suppressing migration; implicated in vasoconstriction
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Can promote vasoconstrictive/pathological responses depending on vascular bed
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in endothelial cells and adult medial VSMCs
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Endothelial cells; vascular smooth muscle; cardiomyocytes
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. S1P2 inhibits Rac in a Rho-dependent manner and antagonizes S1P1-regulated migration. 2. It is expressed in medial VSMCs and associated with reduced migration. 3. Together with S1P3, it is implicated in vasoconstrictive signaling.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Knockout mice; receptor subtype signaling analyses; cell migration assays

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to vasomotor control and cardiovascular signaling
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to vascular and cardiac responses to S1P, including vasoconstrictive signaling
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in endothelial cells, VSMCs, and cardiomyocytes
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Endothelial cells; adult medial and pup intimal VSMCs; cardiomyocytes
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. S1P3 is expressed in vascular and cardiac cells responsive to S1P. 2. Genetic analysis supports a role with S1P2 in regulating PLC/Ca2+ pathways. 3. It is considered a candidate mediator of vasoconstriction.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Null mouse-derived cell signaling analyses; expression studies

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports heart development via S1P signaling
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: S1P receptor-like GPCR, most similar to mammalian S1P2
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for myocardial precursor migration and normal heart tube fusion
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Mutation of the zebrafish S1P receptor Miles Apart causes myocardial precursor migration defects. 2. Loss of function results in cardia bifida. 3. This supports conserved developmental roles of S1P receptor signaling.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Modulates embryonic development and angiogenesis-related processes through S1P dephosphorylation and cross-talk with Wnt signaling
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Lipid phosphate phosphohydrolase family membrane phosphatase
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum; Golgi; lipid raft membrane subdomains
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deletion causes severe embryonic developmental defects; capable of dephosphorylating S1P and downregulating canonical Wnt signaling
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss is embryonic lethal with severe developmental abnormalities
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Mouse LPP3 can dephosphorylate S1P. 2. LPP3 deletion causes severe defects in chorioallantois, placenta, yolk sac vasculature, and body axis formation. 3. LPP3 negatively regulates canonical Wnt/β-catenin signaling.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Controls intracellular and secreted S1P pools and thereby survival signaling
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Intracellular ER-associated S1P pool; extracellular media upon knockdown
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Dephosphorylates S1P/dihydroS1P, lowers S1P levels, promotes ceramide formation and apoptosis; knockdown causes S1P accumulation and secretion and resistance to death stimuli
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression induces apoptosis; knockdown may enhance survival signaling
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. hSPP1 is an ER-localized S1P phosphatase that decreases intracellular S1P and increases ceramide. 2. Overexpression induces apoptosis. 3. siRNA knockdown causes intracellular S1P accumulation, increased S1P secretion, and resistance to TNF-α/daunorubicin-induced death.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression; GFP localization; siRNA knockdown; lipid measurements; apoptosis assays

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Regulates S1P turnover in tissue-specific contexts
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Second human S1P phosphatase isoform with biochemical properties similar to hSPP1 but divergent tissue expression
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Divergent tissue-specific expression relative to hSPP1
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. hSPP2 is a second ER-localized human S1P phosphatase. 2. It is highly similar to hSPP1 biochemically. 3. Distinct tissue expression suggests functional divergence.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes survival, proliferation, migration, vascular signaling
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Intracellular pools; extracellular milieu after export
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Lipid kinase with diacylglycerol kinase-homologous domain; conserved GGKGK motif
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Phosphorylates sphingosine at the C1 hydroxyl using ATP to form S1P
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key regulatory/control step in S1P synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Generates S1P and generally promotes cell survival/proliferation; activation and membrane translocation facilitate extracellular S1P production/signaling
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Increased SK1 activity may enhance inflammatory, proliferative, or atherogenic signaling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Endothelial cells; HEK293 cells; multiple mammalian cell types
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: PDGF; NGF; EGF; TNF-α; bFGF; VEGF; phorbol ester; estrogen; stress conditions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SK1 directly synthesizes S1P from sphingosine. 2. ERK2 phosphorylation at Ser225 and PKC-dependent processes activate SK1 and promote membrane translocation. 3. SK1 interacts with TRAF2 in TNF-α signaling and supports proliferative/survival responses.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Therapeutic target for modulating S1P production, inflammatory signaling, vascular permeability, and proliferation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutagenesis of Gly82 and Ser225; enzyme activity assays; inhibitor studies; translocation assays; protein interaction studies

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Controls S1P production and metabolic activation of FTY720; can promote cell-cycle arrest/apoptosis in overexpression settings
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: S1P synthesis from sphingosine and related long-chain bases
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target product and activates therapeutic analog
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Lipid kinase with conserved SK domains and unique N-terminal extension; nuclear localization sequence
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: S1P; phosphorylated unusual substrates including FTY720-phosphate
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Phosphorylates sphingosine and certain related long-chain bases or analogs to corresponding phosphate products
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key regulatory/control step in specific cellular contexts
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces S1P but functionally differs from SK1; overexpression causes cell-cycle arrest and apoptosis, and SK2 activates FTY720
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Can induce apoptosis/cell-cycle arrest when overexpressed
Pathway_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Genomic and epigenetic regulation suggested as important future area, not established
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SK2 is a distinct mammalian isoform with a unique N-terminus and nuclear localization signal. 2. It phosphorylates FTY720 and unusual sphingoid substrates more efficiently than SK1. 3. SK2 overexpression induces cell-cycle arrest and apoptosis, contrasting with SK1.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important target for therapeutic metabolic activation of FTY720 and selective manipulation of S1P pools
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Cloning; substrate specificity assays; localization studies; overexpression phenotyping

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Extracellular face of plasma membrane interface
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: LPP family membrane phosphatase with conserved KX6RP, PSGH, SRX5HX3D motifs
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Dephosphorylated lipid backbones including sphingosine from S1P
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes phosphate from S1P and other lipid phosphates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely attenuates extracellular S1P receptor signaling by degrading S1P near the cell surface
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad substrate range may affect multiple lipid signaling pathways
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LPP1 is a membrane phosphatase able to dephosphorylate S1P. 2. Its active site faces the extracellular space. 3. It is proposed to dampen lysophospholipid receptor signaling.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for modulating extracellular S1P/LPA signaling

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Human; Mouse; Yeast; Drosophila; Caenorhabditis elegans; Dictyostelium
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Homo sapiens; Mus musculus; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Drosophila melanogaster; Caenorhabditis elegans; Dictyostelium discoideum
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Human; Mouse; Yeast; Drosophila; C. elegans; Dictyostelium
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Homo sapiens; Mus musculus; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Drosophila melanogaster; Caenorhabditis elegans; Caenorhabditis elegans; Dictyostelium discoideum
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Irreversible elimination of S1P, links sphingolipid metabolism to phospholipid, sterol, and fatty acid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Irreversibly degrades target product and channels metabolites into other lipid pathways
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Irreversibly cleaves S1P at the C2–C3 bond to form a long-chain aldehyde and ethanolamine phosphate
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Major control point for irreversible S1P disposal
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduces intracellular S1P, promotes stress-induced ceramide generation and apoptosis, and coordinates broader lipid metabolic flux
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression induces apoptosis; loss causes S1P accumulation and can alter development, stress resistance, and drug resistance
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Present in all mammalian tissues examined except platelets
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Serine palmitoyltransferase (functional metabolic coupling); ceramide synthase (functional dependence in apoptosis assays)
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Serum deprivation; heat stress; nutrient deprivation; retinoic acid; cisplatin; developmental and nutritional conditions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. S1P lyase irreversibly degrades S1P and is ER-localized. 2. Loss- and gain-of-function studies across species show major roles in development, survival, stress response, and drug response. 3. Human enzyme activity is required for apoptosis induction and for coordinating S1P with ceramide and broader lipid metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Therapeutic target for diseases involving dysregulated apoptosis, inflammation, and lipid homeostasis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene knockout/mutants; overexpression; GFP localization; catalytic-site mutagenesis (K353, cysteines); apoptosis assays; metabolite analyses

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Links TNF-α signaling to S1P-dependent inflammatory and survival outputs
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Associates with SK1 during TNF-α signaling
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to TNF-α-regulated SK1 activation linked to NF-κB activation and apoptosis inhibition
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent controller linking inflammatory signaling to S1P generation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TNF-α induces interaction between TRAF2 and SK1. 2. This coupling contributes to NF-κB activation and anti-apoptotic signaling. 3. TRAF2 therefore links cytokine input to S1P metabolic signaling.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Stimulates inducible S1P production and release
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Phosphorylates human SK1 at Ser225, increasing activity and membrane translocation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances SK1 catalytic activity and membrane translocation, likely promoting extracellular S1P release
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Direct activator of inducible SK1-dependent S1P formation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ERK2 phosphorylates SK1 at Ser225. 2. This increases SK1 activity and promotes membrane translocation. 3. ERK2 thereby supports stimulus-induced S1P production/release.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes stimulus-coupled S1P synthesis and export
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Activation and translocation of S1P synthesis machinery
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates SK1 and mediates its translocation from cytosol to plasma membrane
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes VEGF-stimulated SK1 activation, membrane translocation, and S1P appearance in media
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Stimulus-dependent activator of SK1-driven S1P production/export
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PKC activates SK1 in response to VEGF. 2. PKC-dependent signaling drives SK1 translocation to the plasma membrane. 3. This is associated with increased extracellular S1P.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Activation/translocation assays in endothelial and HEK293 cells
